channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Tagging, 3rd March
item.description:
Tagging

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Chailey Avenue, Enfield

item.category: Inoffensive up to 2 metres off the ground

item.title: Fly tipping, 3rd March
item.description:
Fly tipping down their own alleyway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Iron Mill Lane, Barnes Cray, Greater London

item.title: Furniture in the pavement, 3rd March
item.description:
Someone is leaving furniture close to our recycling bins. I’ve already cleaned and disposed what left so many times but people are still leaving old furniture outside in the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 13, Ancaster Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox in toad, 3rd March
item.description:
Moved to outside our house 71a Malmains Way
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, Brabourne Rise, Park Langley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More dumped rubbish, 3rd March
item.description:
Rubbish has been dumped again as well as now accumulating in the alley way. The council contacted me before saying it would be removed but it is still there.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parkside Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged dropped curb, 3rd March
item.description:
Hello, we had a dropped curb installed last year and we currently have road closures and works taking pace down our road. I came home from work one day and found that our dropped curb had been damaged. This is outside of number 9 Saxville Road BR5 3AN. Please can you assist in getting this repaired. Many thanks, Hannah

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Saxville Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Car being repaired on highway, 3rd March
item.description:
Car parked creating access issues. Parked for 2.months with movement and now wheels off and on jack's since Sunday. Causes issues for passing cars and access to driveway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Canada Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): York Grove, Nunhead, Greater London

item.title: Builders waste and van blocking pavement, 3rd March
item.description:
32 Royal Hill - builders are blocking pavement and road - has been there all day and this evening also forcing people onto a busy road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Royal Hill, Greenwich

item.title: Lights not working on Thames path, 3rd March
item.description:

item.title: Multiple large potholes, 3rd March
item.description:
Submitted this form yesterday, for the case to be closed almost immediately with "case closed - fixed". The potholes are very much still present and this road is dangerous. There are multiple along this whole stretch of road!! These have not been fixed as stated

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Rectory Lane, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Open to abuse from motorised vehicles, 3rd March
item.description:
Trial bikes etc get through Dangerous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 137, Norfolk Crescent, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London

item.title: Daily fly tipping, 3rd March
item.description:
Daily fly tipping at this location

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mackenzie Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside arada market, the green shop, 3rd March
item.description:
Please, the disabled bay is faded and it’s nearly impossible to park there now, people treat it as a normal bay and do not allow actual blue badges to park as they treat it as one continuous bay. They park too close to my car when I park in the bay and I’ve had someone hit my car because of the lack of space they leave trying to squeeze into such a small space. Please repaint the disabled bay so people can see how much space needs to be left and allow those to park without worrying their car will be damaged. It’s bee like this for years.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Malvern Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
